info.name: VectorWave
info.comment: Extends the Vector class with support for sine wave based paths.

description.p: The VectorWave class provides functionality for generating paths based on sine waves. This feature is not part of the SVG standard and therefore should not be used in cases where SVG compliance is a strict requirement.

The sine wave will be generated from (X,Y) across Length. The parent view height provides the vertical region for centring and path closure, while Amplitude defines the wave height. The path vertices are generated on a left-to-right basis.
Waves can be used in Kotuku's SVG implementation by using the <kotuku:wave/> element.

action.name: Move
action.comment: Moves the vector to a new position.
action.prototype: ERR acMove(*Object, DOUBLE DeltaX, DOUBLE DeltaY, DOUBLE DeltaZ)

input.param: The number of units to move along the X axis.
The number of units to move along the Y axis.
The number of units to move along the Z axis.

action.name: MoveToPoint
action.comment: Moves the vector to a new fixed position.
action.prototype: ERR acMoveToPoint(*Object, DOUBLE X, DOUBLE Y, DOUBLE Z, MTF Flags)

input.param: The new X position to move the object to.
The new Y position to move the object to.
The new Z position to move the object to.
Set the relevant MTF flag for each provided parameter.

action.name: Resize
action.comment: Changes the vector's length and amplitude.
action.prototype: ERR acResize(*Object, DOUBLE Width, DOUBLE Height, DOUBLE Depth)

input.param: The new width of the object.
The new height of the object.
The new depth of the object.

description.p: The Amplitude is expressed as a fixed or scaled value. Fixed values are used directly, while scaled values are resolved against the parent view height.

field.name: Close
field.comment: Closes the generated wave path at either the top or bottom.

description.p: Setting the Close field to TOP or BOTTOM will close the generated wave's path so that it is suitable for being filled. This setting is not compatible with the Thickness option.

field.name: Decay
field.comment: Declares a rate of decay to apply to the wave amplitude.

description.p: The amplitude of a wave can be decayed between its start and end points by setting the Decay field. A value of 1.0 is the default and applies no decay. Values between 0.0 and 1.0 set the amplitude multiplier at the end of the wave, so 0.5 tapers the wave to half amplitude and 0.999 remains visually close to no decay. If the value is negative, the start and end points for the decay will be reversed.

field.name: Envelope
field.comment: Selects the decay envelope applied to the wave amplitude.

description.p: The Envelope field controls how the Decay value is interpolated across the wave. The default LINEAR envelope uses an even taper, while QUADRATIC, SMOOTHSTEP and EXPONENTIAL apply progressively stronger curves to the decay.

field.name: Frequency
field.comment: Defines the wave frequency (the distance between each wave).

description.p: The frequency determines the distance between each individual wave that is generated. The default value for the frequency is 1.0. Shortening the frequency to a value closer to 0 will bring the waves closer together.

field.name: Length
field.comment: The horizontal length of the generated wave.

description.p: The length of the generated wave is defined here as a fixed or scaled value.

field.name: Phase
field.comment: Declares the initial phase, in degrees, to use when generating the wave.

description.p: The phase value defines the initial position that is used when computing the wave. The default is zero.
Visually, changing the phase will affect the 'offset' of the generated wave. Gradually incrementing the value will give the wave an appearance of moving from right to left.

field.name: Thickness
field.comment: Expands the diameter of the wave to the specified value to produce a closed path.

description.p: Specifying a thickness value will create a wave that forms a filled shape, rather than the default of a stroked path. The thickness (diameter) of the wave is determined by the provided value. If defined as a percentage, the value is resolved against the normalised diagonal of the parent viewport, matching the scaling rule used for #StrokeWidth. Thickness is not compatible with the Close option, and takes precedence over it.

By default, waves are generated in the style of a sine wave. Alternative styles can be selected by setting this field. The SMOOTH style generates a sine wave, TRIANGLE generates a triangular wave and SAWTOOTH generates a ramp with a sharp return edge.

field.name: X
field.comment: The x coordinate of the wave. Can be expressed as a fixed or scaled coordinate.

description.p: The x coordinate of the wave is defined here as either a fixed or scaled value.

field.name: Y
field.comment: The y coordinate of the wave. Can be expressed as a fixed or scaled coordinate.

description.p: The y coordinate of the wave is defined here as either a fixed or scaled value.

constants.const: Close the path across the bottom of its area.
Close the path across the top of its area.
Exponential decay, weighted strongly towards the end of the wave.
Linear decay from start to end, this is the default.
Quadratic decay, weighted towards the end of the wave.
Smoothstep decay with ease-in and ease-out.
Sawtooth patterns rise at 45 degrees to the peak, then direct 90 degrees to the bottom.
Standard sine-wave curvature, this is the default.
Chevron style angles at 45 degrees either side of the peak and bottom edge.
